perturbation.original_text: Based on the foregoing, and subject to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement, the Agents severally and not jointly agree to act as, and the Corporation appoints the Agents as, the exclusive agents of the Corporation to offer the Offered Shares for sale on the Closing Date (as defined herein) in the Selling Jurisdictions (as defined herein) on a private placement basis at the Offering Price.
perturbation.changed_text: Based on the foregoing, and subject to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement, the Agents agree to act as, and the Corporation appoints the Agents as, non-exclusive agents of the Corporation to offer the Offered Shares for sale on the Closing Date (as defined herein) in the Selling Jurisdictions (as defined herein) on a private placement basis at the Offering Price. The Corporation reserves the right to appoint other agents or underwriters for concurrent offerings.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'exclusive agents' to 'non-exclusive agents' and adding that 'The Corporation reserves the right to appoint other agents or underwriters for concurrent offerings,' creates an in-text contradiction. The original agreement structure ensured the Agents had a sole privilege which is no longer being followed and now it is creating uncertainty to the enforcement.
perturbation.location: Face page of Exhibit 1.3 AGENCY AGREEMENT
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Corporation agrees that the Agents are acting as agents of the Corporation in seeking purchasers of Offered Shares without underwriter liability, and nothing in this Agreement or any other agreement shall require the Agents to purchase any of the Offered Shares in connection with the Offering.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the Agents as 'exclusive agents,' implying they were the sole entities responsible for selling the Offered Shares. The changed text modifies this to 'non-exclusive agents' and explicitly allows the Corporation to appoint other agents. This directly contradicts the understanding in the contradicted text, which states 'the Agents are acting as agents of the Corporation in seeking purchasers of Offered Shares without underwriter liability.' With the change, other agents can also be appointed, creating a contradiction because the original text implied exclusivity, and the new text denies this exclusivity.

perturbation.original_text: (a) the opinions, certificates and agreements referred to in Section 9 and all other documents required to be provided by the Corporation to the Agents pursuant to this Agreement and the Subscription Agreements; (b) except for any Offered Shares offered or sold in the United States which shall be represented by individual definitive share certificates, one or more definitive share certificate(s)/and or book-entry only securities, duly registered as the Lead Agent may direct the Corporation;
perturbation.changed_text: (a) the opinions, certificates and agreements referred to in Section 9 and all other documents required to be provided by the Corporation to the Agents pursuant to this Agreement and the Subscription Agreements; (b) all Offered Shares shall be represented exclusively by book-entry only securities, regardless of where they are offered or sold; individual definitive share certificates are not permitted;
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows for individual definitive share certificates for shares sold in the United States, but the modified clause explicitly prohibits them and mandates 'book-entry only securities' across the board. This in-text contradiction creates confusion about what form the shares should take which can affect how the agreement is enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The sale of the Offered Shares shall be completed at the Closing Time at the offices of Corporation's Counsel in Toronto, Ontario or at such other place as the Corporation and the Agents may agree. At the Closing Time, the Corporation shall deliver to the Agents: (a) the opinions, certificates and agreements referred to in Section 9 and all other documents required to be provided by the Corporation to the Agents pursuant to this Agreement and the Subscription Agreements; (b) all Offered Shares shall be represented exclusively by book-entry only securities, regardless of where they are offered or sold; individual definitive share certificates are not permitted; (c) the Corporation's receipt for payment by the Agents of an amount equal to the aggregate purchase price for the Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering; and (d) such further documentation as may be contemplated by this Agreement or as Agents' Counsel or the applicable regulatory authorities may reasonably require; against delivery by the Agents of: 16 (e) all duly completed Subscription Agreements tendered by the Subscribers for the Offered Shares being issued and sold and, where applicable, all completed forms, schedules and certificates contemplated by such Subscription Agreements; (f) a wire transfer of immediately available funds in an amount equal to the aggregate purchase price for the Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering, less an amount equal to the Agents' Fee and the costs and expenses of the Agent provided for in Section 14; and (g) the Agents' receipt for the Agents' Fee and the definitive certificates delivered to the Agents in accordance with Section 6(b).
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for individual definitive share certificates for shares sold in the United States, while the changed text explicitly prohibits them and mandates 'book-entry only securities' across the board. The contradicted text includes the changed text which prohibits individual definitive share certificates, while the original text allowed it. This creates a direct conflict and confusion about the share certificates within the same section, directly contradicting its original allowance.

perturbation.original_text: All expenses payable by the Corporation to the Agent in accordance with this Agreement shall be payable whether or not the Offering is completed.
perturbation.changed_text: All expenses payable by the Corporation to the Agent in accordance with this Agreement shall only be payable if the Offering is successfully completed and the Closing occurs. If the Offering is terminated for any reason prior to the Closing, the Corporation shall not be liable for any expenses.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause says all expenses are payable regardless of completion, while the modified one conditions payment on successful completion and explicitly states no liability if terminated. This creates an in-text contradiction about when expenses are payable, making enforcement of this clause more difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Whether or not the transactions contemplated by this Agreement shall be completed, all expenses of or incidental to the issue, sale and delivery of the Offered Shares and all expenses of or incidental to all other matters in connection with the offering of the Offered Shares shall be borne by the Corporation including, without limitation, all fees and disbursements of all legal counsel to the Corporation (including U.S., foreign and local counsel), all fees and disbursements of the Corporation's accountants and auditors, all expenses related to road shows and marketing activities, all printing costs incurred in connection with the offering of the Offered Shares, including certificates, if any, representing the Offered Shares, all filing fees, all fees and expenses relating to listing the Offered Shares on any exchanges, all transfer agent fees and expenses, and all reasonable out-of-pocket expenses of the Agents incurred in connection with the offering of the Offered Shares, including without limitation the fees of Canadian counsel to the Agents which shall not exceed $50,000 (not including applicable taxes and disbursements and $10,000 USD plus tax and disbursements for U.S. Counsel), and any advertising, printing, courier, telecommunications, data search, presentation, travel and other expenses incurred by the Agents together with all related taxes (including, without limitation, provincial sales taxes and HST).
perturbation.justification: The original text states expenses are payable regardless of the offering's completion. The changed text makes expense payment contingent on successful completion and denies liability if the offering terminates early. This directly contradicts the provided Contradicted Text which states that all expenses shall be borne by the corporation, 'Whether or not the transactions contemplated by this Agreement shall be completed'. The changed text introduces a condition that directly opposes the unconditional expense coverage outlined in the Contradicted Text.
